---
title: "Carbon Intensity: 20-Year vs 100-Year Horizon"
format:
  html:
    self-contained: true
  pdf:
    fig-width: 8
    fig-height: 5
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: inline
---

```{r setup}
#| warning: false
#| message: false
knitr::opts_chunk$set(fig.width = 8, fig.height = 5, dpi = 150)
library(tidyverse)
library(patchwork)
```

```{r load}
#| warning: false
#| message: false
# Li & He (2022) Table S3 — median CI (kg CO2e/MWh) at 20-year and 100-year GWP horizon
trophic_data <- tibble(
  Category = factor(c("Oligotrophic", "Mesotrophic", "Eutrophic"),
                    levels = c("Oligotrophic", "Mesotrophic", "Eutrophic")),
  CI_20yr  = c(67.2, 116.0, 248.0),
  CI_100yr = c(55.6, 69.1, 116.7)
)

depth_data <- tibble(
  Category = factor(c("Shallow (<20 m)", "Medium (20–100 m)", "Deep (>100 m)"),
                    levels = c("Deep (>100 m)", "Medium (20–100 m)", "Shallow (<20 m)")),
  CI_20yr  = c(233.0, 114.3, 35.6),
  CI_100yr = c(124.5, 66.0, 27.7)
)

trophic_long <- trophic_data %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("CI"), names_to = "Horizon", values_to = "CI") %>%
  mutate(Horizon = factor(if_else(Horizon == "CI_20yr", "20-Year", "100-Year"), levels = c("20-Year", "100-Year")))

depth_long <- depth_data %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("CI"), names_to = "Horizon", values_to = "CI") %>%
  mutate(Horizon = factor(if_else(Horizon == "CI_20yr", "20-Year", "100-Year"), levels = c("20-Year", "100-Year")))
```

```{r plot}
#| warning: false
#| message: false
# Build a “scenario” grid using Li & He (2022) Table S3 marginal medians (20-year horizon).
# NOTE: Li & He report medians separately by trophic and by depth; this is an index (sum of marginals),
# not an observed joint CI for each (depth × trophic) combination.

trophic_20 <- trophic_data %>%
  transmute(trophic = as.character(Category), ci_trophic_20 = CI_20yr)

depth_20 <- depth_data %>%
  transmute(depth = as.character(Category), ci_depth_20 = CI_20yr)

scenario_grid <- tidyr::expand_grid(depth_20, trophic_20) %>%
  mutate(
    ci_index_20 = ci_depth_20 + ci_trophic_20,
    depth = factor(depth, levels = c("Deep (>100 m)", "Medium (20–100 m)", "Shallow (<20 m)")),
    trophic = factor(trophic, levels = c("Oligotrophic", "Mesotrophic", "Eutrophic"))
  )

ggplot(scenario_grid, aes(x = trophic, y = depth, fill = ci_index_20)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(ci_index_20)), color = "white", fontface = "bold", size = 3.5) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "magma", 
    direction = 1,        # 1 = Dark to Light (standard), -1 = Light to Dark
    begin = 0.1,          # Trims the extreme black so it's not too heavy
    end = 0.9,            # Trims the extreme white so the yellow isn't "blown out"
    name = "Index CI\n(kg CO₂e/MWh)") +
  labs(
    title = "Scenario grid (20-year horizon): depth × trophic",
    subtitle = "Index = CI(depth, 20y) + CI(trophic, 20y). Worst = shallow + eutrophic; best = deep + oligotrophic.",
    x = "Trophic state",
    y = "Reservoir depth",
    caption = "Source: Li & He (2022) Table S3 (20-year horizon). Grid values are an index built from marginal medians, not observed joint CI."
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 9, color = "gray40"),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 8, color = "gray50")
  )
```

**Takeaway:** This 3×3 “scenario” grid visualizes all depth × trophic combinations using a simple index (sum of Li & He’s 20-year marginal medians). It highlights that shallow + eutrophic conditions are highest-risk, while deep + oligotrophic conditions are lowest-risk.
